hint: Think about what world(s) this statement is claiming something about. Is it all worlds, some worlds, or the actual world?
explanation: Entailment is NECESSARY implication, □(P ⊃ S). To deny entailment you negate the whole thing: ∼□(P ⊃ S) — it is not necessary that P implies S, so some possible world has P true and S false.
